data.title: Hillenbrand downgraded at KeyBanc on tariff worries, weak pricing power
data.description:
Hillenbrand (NYSE:HI) late Wednesday was downgraded to Sector Weight from a previous investment rating of Overweight by analysts at KeyBanc Capital Markets who cited a confluence of macro and company-specific challenges that are likely to weigh on growth and profitability into fiscal 2026.

In a note to clients, analyst Jeff Hammond pointed to rising demand uncertainty tied to tariffs in Hillenbrand’s Advanced Process Solutions (APS) segment, alongside weak pricing power in its Molding Technology Solutions (MTS) business and an elevated debt load.

“While acknowledging the portfolio enhancements made over the last several years, we feel recent demand uncertainty created by the current tariff regime in APS and the likelihood of sales declines in fiscal 2026 create an overhang that is difficult to ignore,” Hammond wrote. “Paired with weak pricing power in MTS and a high leverage profile in the current environment, we find it prudent to step away.”

APS recovery timeline under pressure

Hammond noted that fiscal 2025 was initially viewed as a transitional year for the APS division, with full testing labs previously seen as a signal of pent-up demand. However, he warned that the introduction of new tariffs has disrupted purchasing behavior, casting doubt on near-term growth.

“We think FY26 could be setting up for sales declines as well,” he added, citing weaker-than-expected order rates in the fiscal second quarter and anticipated declines in the third quarter following post-Liberation Day disruptions.

Margins under strain amid tariff effect

Hillenbrand’s (NYSE:HI) pricing flexibility -- or lack thereof -- has become a growing concern, particularly in the wake of intensified competition and new cost pressures.

“Management does not believe it has the pricing power to cover additional costs from tariffs -- about $7.5 million in fiscal 2025 -- which makes Hillenbrand (NYSE:HI) a negative outlier in our group,” Hammond said.

KeyBanc is now modeling second-half fiscal 2025 margins at just 14.5%, far below historical norms in the low-to-mid 20% range.

Although the firm believes margins could rebound if volumes recover, macro indicators such as declining consumer confidence and continued tariff uncertainty are limiting visibility.

Debt overhang remains

Leverage is also a sticking point. With much of the company’s free cash flow linked to APS performance, Hammond said that any delays in order recovery will make debt reduction more difficult.

“Pro-forma leverage is currently around 3.4 times (and likely to move higher),” he warned. “We expect leverage to remain well above the targeted range of 1.7times to 2.7 times through the end of fiscal 2026.”

Hillenbrand (HI) took several steps to improve its balance sheet -- selling a majority interest in Milacron, divesting TerraSource and raising roughly $365 million -- but KeyBanc’s analysts say investors are unlikely to reengage until the APS business shows signs of a sustained turnaround.

data.title: Silicon Valley execs to push for softer AI regulatory approach in Senate hearing
data.description:
Top executives from leading technology and artificial intelligence companies will appear before Congress on Thursday, presenting a wish list that prioritizes rolling back regulations they claim hinder their growth and inadvertently shift business to China.

“The United States needs a smart export control strategy that protects our national security, while assuring other countries that they will have reliable and sustained access to critical American AI components and services,” Brad Smith, vice chair and president of Microsoft (NASDAQ:MSFT), plans to testify, according to a Bloomberg report citing his prepared remarks.

Smith, along with OpenAI Chief Executive Officer Sam Altman, Lisa Su, the chair and chief executive officer of Advanced Micro Devices (NASDAQ:AMD), and Michael Intrator, co-founder and chief executive officer of CoreWeave (NASDAQ:CRWV), will appear before the Senate Committee on Commerce, Science, and Transportation to advocate for government support in the global race for AI leadership, the report said.

The Trump administration is reportedly set to kill the so-called AI Diffusion rule as part of a broader plan to alter semiconductor trade restrictions.

“As AI systems become more capable, people will want to use them even more. Meeting that demand requires more chips, training data, energy and supercomputers,” Altman is expected to say in his prepared testimony, according to the report.

The Biden administration followed policies such as safety testing requirements for AI systems. However, Silicon Valley executives are pressing Congress to scale back those regulations.

In his testimony, Smith plans to reiterate a common concern among tech executives: that limiting the export of U.S. AI chips to certain countries could push foreign firms toward Chinese alternatives.

In August 2023, the Biden administration imposed export control curbs on advanced semiconductors, like those made by Nvidia (NVDA), to some countries in the Middle East. Nvidia warned about the restrictions in a filing with the Securities and Exchange Commission.

data.title: Chunghwa Telecom reports Q1 results
data.description:
• Chunghwa Telecom press release (NYSE:CHT): Q1 Total revenue increased by 1.6% to NT$ 55.81 billion.
  • Consumer Business Group revenue decreased by 0.2% to NT$ 34.57 billion.
  • Enterprise Business Group revenue increased by 2.3% to NT$ 17.34 billion.
  • International Business Group revenue increased by 0.9% to NT$ 2.44 billion.
  • Total operating costs and expenses increased by 0.6% to NT$ 43.29 billion.
  • Operating income increased by 5.0% to NT$ 12.52 billion.
  • EBITDA increased by 3.2% to NT$ 22.53 billion.
  • Net income attributable to stockholders of the parent increased by 4.3% to NT$ 9.80 billion.
  • Basic earnings per share was NT$1.26.
  • Operating income, income before tax, net income attributable to stockholders of the parent and EPS all exceeded our proposed guidance.

data.title: Vor Bio plunges as it winds down all manufacturing operations, reduces 95% of workforce
data.description:
• Vor Bio (NASDAQ:VOR) said on Thursday that based on currently available clinical data from its key clinical programs and a challenging fundraising environment, the board has approved the initiation of a process to explore a range of strategic alternatives. 
  • These strategic alternatives could include, among other alternatives, a potential sale of assets of the company, a potential licensing of assets of the company, a sale of the company, a business combination, a merger or other strategic action.
  • The company is also winding down its clinical and manufacturing operations, beginning immediately, including its ongoing clinical trials. The decision to discontinue clinical operations is not due to any safety concerns with Vor Bio’s product candidates.
  • With the strategic process, Vor Bio implemented a workforce reduction of approximately 95% with a cost of approximately $10.9 million.
  • Vor Bio plans to retain approximately 8 employees to assist in exploring its strategic alternatives, and winding-down the clinical and manufacturing operations.
  • Vor Bio (NASDAQ:VOR) shares were down more than 44% in premarket trading.

data.title: Kiwetinohk Energy GAAP EPS of $1.23; updates FY25 guidance
data.description:
• Kiwetinohk Energy press release (TSX:KEC:CA): Q1 GAAP EPS of $1.23.
  • Record quarterly production of 32,611 boe/d, increased 18% over the fourth quarter (53% natural gas + 47% condensate and NGLs) with three new Duvernay wells and one Simonette Montney well brought on stream.
  • Generated $29.5 million in free cash flow3 on capital expenditures (before acquisitions/dispositions)3 of $86.4 million. Free cash flow supports the capital development program and debt repayments.
  • Reduced net debt to annualized adjusted funds flow from operations ratio4 by 25% from year end 2024 levels, exiting the quarter with a ratio of 0.75x.

  • Guidance update

    Kiwetinohk has reduced annual guidance for operating costs and transportation costs as a result of strong performance during the first quarter of 2025.

    • Projected operating costs have been reduced by $0.50/boe to a revised annual target range of $6.75 - $7.25/boe.
    • Projected transportation costs have been reduced by $0.25/boe to a revised annual target range of $5.75 - $6.00/boe.

data.title: First Advantage Non-GAAP EPS of $0.17 beats by $0.04, revenue of $354.6M beats by $10.36M
data.description:
• First Advantage press release (NASDAQ:FA): Q1 Non-GAAP EPS of $0.17 beats by $0.04.
  • Revenue of $354.6M (+109.3% Y/Y) beats by $10.36M.
  • Shares -9.49% PM.

  • Reaffirming Full Year 2025 Guidance

    • Reaffirming full year 2025 guidance ranges, including the expected benefits of realized synergies, for Revenues of $1.5 billion to $1.6 billion, Adjusted EBITDA of $410 million to $450 million, Adjusted Net Income of $152 million to $182 million, and Adjusted Diluted Earnings Per Share of $0.86 to $1.03
  • FY EPS consensus of $0.90
  • FY revenue consensus of $1.51B

data.title: Canadian Utilities appoints CEO, executive chair
data.description:
• Canadian Utilities Limited (TSX:CU:CA) has appointed Bob Myles as the chief executive officer and Nancy Southern as the executive chair of the company.
  • Both changes are effective May 8, 2025. 
  • Myles previously held the role of president and chief operating officer, Canadian Utilities.
  • Southern was first named chief executive officer of Canadian Utilities in 2000.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: BitFuFu produces 209 BTC in April
data.description:
• BitFuFu (NASDAQ:FUFU) announced 1,908 bitcoin (BTC) held in April, an increase of 61 BTC from March 31, 2025, attributed to self-mining operations and net Bitcoin inflow from customer payments.
  • 209 BTC produced in April 2025, including 173 BTC from cloud mining and 36 BTC from self-mining.
  • Total hashrate under management reached 28.3 EH/s as of April 30, 2025, a 37.4% month-over-month increase.
  • Total power capacity under management rose to 566 MW across five continents as of April 30, 2025, an 18.4% month-over-month increase.
  • FUFU shares up 2.9% premarket on Thursday.
  • Source: Press release
